refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to minimize packet loss.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to ensure packet delivery.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to minimize packet loss.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication adaptation and teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring in future refinements to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation control and communication management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time network and system status data to monitor communication quality.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation communication specifics.
Physical vehicle components have minimal direct role in packet loss management.
Primary responsibility for teleoperation communication quality; best containment.
Safety alerts may be affected but not responsible for packet loss control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution module if communication management is partially implemented there.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface ownership and responsibilities between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules to avoid duplication.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear performance focus and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Minor risk of duplicated communication logic if NGHE Solution also implements related functions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and appropriate interface usage.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for packet loss management to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules to prevent functional overlap.
Consider adding latency monitoring in future FR refinements.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the requirements with acceptable risk and clear ownership. Verification methods are feasible and interfaces are well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; next step is to decompose sub-FRs for detailed design.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive bitrate algorithms effectively reduce packet loss without excessive latency.
open_risks: Potential latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness.
Interface ownership clarity between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
evidence_needed: Performance data from adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms under realistic network conditions.
Interface specification documents between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.
